title: Use Map.fetch + with for required-key access pattern
prompt: Given: `def endpoint(config) do url = config[:url]; api_key = config[:api_key]; if url && api_key, do: {:ok, url <> "?key=" <> api_key}, else: {:error, :missing_config} end`. Rewrite using a `with` expression + `Map.fetch/2` so missing keys trigger the error path via pattern-match short-circuit. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.ApiClient do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Two transforms (dynamic access → fetch, truthy-check → with). Sonnet often does one.
